title: Contest creation still lands in DRAFT instead of going live for entries
description: The implemented contest flow still assumes an intermediate DRAFT state, which conflicts with the current product requirement that a contest becomes live as soon as the commissioner creates it. Backend contest creation writes ContestStatus.DRAFT, functional tests still expect DRAFT, create-contest page says configuration is editable while still in draft, and contest detail only allows entry creation when status === OPEN. This is a core blocker for the commissioner->member flow. Evidence: packages/core-api/src/modules/contests/service.ts lines 79-84 create contests with ContestStatus.DRAFT; tests/functional/contests.functional.ts lines 149-182 assert DRAFT after creation; clients/poolmaster/src/features/contests/create-contest-page.tsx lines 892-903 and clients/poolmaster/src/features/contests/contest-detail-page.tsx lines 391-395 still gate behavior around DRAFT vs OPEN.
close_reason: The commissioner web flow uses createManagedContest, which already creates OPEN contests. This issue was a triage misdiagnosis based on the generic ContestService path, not the active webapp path.

title: Add root-admin event sync surface
description: Extend the existing /root-admin operational page so root admins can manually trigger sport-level event sync from the webapp using the existing backend sync contract. This is an exceptional operational unblock/testing surface, not a new day-to-day admin workflow.
notes: Implementing route rename to /manage and extending the existing root-admin page with manual sport sync plus sync history in the same operational section.

title: Entry builder is not implemented for non-tiered contests
description: The entry page hard-stops for any selection type other than TIERED and shows a not-ready message instead of a usable flow. That leaves category picks and any other supported contest types without a member entry path in the current web app. Evidence: clients/poolmaster/src/features/contests/contest-entry-page.tsx lines 491-504 return a blocking 'This entry flow is not ready yet' state whenever contest.selectionType !== 'TIERED'.
close_reason: The first-pass web create flow is now constrained to supported tiered golf contests only, so commissioners can no longer create a non-tiered contest that lacks an entry builder in the web app.

title: Contest setup does not show event field or participant readiness
description: The commissioner create-contest page lets the user pick an event, but it does not display any imported participant field, participant count, readiness signal, or field preview before contest creation. That makes it impossible to verify whether the selected event actually has usable participant data, which matches the current report that no participant data is visible for event fields. Evidence: clients/poolmaster/src/features/contests/create-contest-page.tsx lines 958-975 only render an event select, and the surrounding summary sidebar at lines 1331-1365 shows event name/start/locks but no participant field data.
close_reason: Create contest now surfaces event participant count, readiness status, release/field lock times, and contest-ready eligibility so commissioners can see event-field readiness before creating a contest.

title: Contest and history navigation is still non-functional
description: The authenticated app shell still renders Contest List and Standings & History as disabled buttons instead of working routes, and /contests still goes to a PlaceholderPage instead of a usable member surface. This leaves core contest discovery and completed-contest history inaccessible from the main navigation. Evidence: clients/poolmaster/src/features/app-shell/app-shell.tsx lines 135-150 render disabled nav buttons, and clients/poolmaster/src/routes/index.tsx lines 95-102 route /contests to PlaceholderPage.
close_reason: Enabled top-level contest/history navigation from the app shell and removed the /contests placeholder route so those paths are no longer dead ends.

title: League selector menu stays open over page content
description: The active league switcher is implemented as a raw <details> dropdown with an absolutely positioned panel and no controlled close behavior. It remains open after navigation or other interactions, which matches the current user report that menus stay expanded over forms. Evidence: clients/poolmaster/src/features/app-shell/league-selector.tsx uses <details> at lines 28-90 with no state, outside-click handling, or close-on-select logic.
close_reason: Replaced the raw <details> league selector with a controlled popover that closes on selection, outside click, and Escape, so it no longer stays open over page content.

title: Implement core-api mock provider binding
description: Add environment-driven sports data provider binding config, register the QA mock provider adapter when selected, and keep existing adapters as the default path elsewhere.
notes: Completed environment-driven provider binding consumption in core-api with a mock contest feed adapter, env parsing, registry wiring, and focused unit verification.

title: Implement event operational timing and readiness
description: Add the event-level PoolMaster operational layer for releaseAt, fieldLocksAt, readiness evaluation, and contest-eligibility signaling on top of provider-discovered SportEvent records.
notes: Completed event operational timing/readiness foundation: SportEvent releaseAt/fieldLocksAt, timing policy model, readiness mapping, ingestion resolution, events DTO/mapper updates, and focused verification.

title: Implement released contest field projection and entry reads
description: Persist the frozen contest-specific field projection at contest creation and switch contest entry/edit flows to read that frozen projection instead of live event valuations/orderings.
notes: Implementing contest leaderboard/detail read flow and related live contest surfaces.
close_reason: Expanded contest entry detail route, standings-based leaderboard UI, and related tests are implemented and green locally.

title: Implement seeded contest config templates and create flow
description: Add the first-class seeded ContestConfigTemplate model, template selection on contest creation, template provenance on contest configuration, and create-time copy/override behavior.
notes: Depends on the event timing/readiness slice for a clean commissioner create flow. After that, this becomes the next critical-path slice.
close_reason: completed

title: Fix root-admin user DTO drift breaking CI
description: Root-admin admin user list/detail responses drifted from UserProfileDto and started returning incomplete or incorrectly mapped fields, causing the service coverage workflow to fail in tests/functional/root-admin.functional.ts. Fix the production contract and add happy-path contract verification so future DTO drift is caught before CI.
notes: Production fix implemented locally: admin user list/detail now include username and map timeFormat/dateFormat to public DTO enums; added happy-path root-admin contract verification.
close_reason: Fixed in commit ea6cde2: admin root-admin user reads now match UserProfileDto and happy-path contract verification covers the regression.

title: CEFI-003 Contest release readiness
description: Clarify whether contest creation itself should make the contest immediately live for entries or whether a separate intermediate commissioner release step is required.
close_reason: Resolved: there is no separate intermediate release step in the normal flow; once the commissioner completes setup and creates the contest, it is immediately entry-ready.

title: CEFI-001 Event creation authority
description: Decide whether first-pass root-admin event operations should only sync/authorize provider-discovered events or also support PoolMaster-originated manual event stubs.
close_reason: Resolved: provider-discovered events only; root admin controls releaseAt and fieldLocksAt rather than manually creating events.

title: CEFI-004 Contest field freeze boundary
description: Define which derived contest-field attributes freeze at contest release and which continue to refresh until contest lock.
close_reason: Resolved: released contests freeze their derived contest field at release so later provider updates do not invalidate existing entries.

title: CEFI-006 Pre-lock participant withdrawal handling
description: Define how PoolMaster should handle already-selected participants who withdraw or become unavailable before contest lock.
close_reason: Resolved: pre-lock participant status changes are informational only; existing entries do not auto-change and members remain responsible for edits until contest lock.

title: CEFI-002 Contest eligibility readiness
description: Define the minimum event readiness required before a commissioner may create a contest and before a contest may be released for entries.
close_reason: Resolved: commissioners may create contests as soon as the event exists; members may create and edit entries before field lock up to contest lock.

title: Contest event feed integration design
description: Track the cross-module product and technical refinement for admin event operations, mock sports data provider evolution, commissioner contest setup/release, and team contest entry built on the event-derived contest field.
notes: Core contest event feed integration design and first-pass implementation are complete. Any remaining deeper mock-provider scoring-correction realism is a follow-on hardening enhancement and not active delivery work.

title: Map active module lanes to Beads epics
description: Seed or align Beads epics for the active multi-module lanes so concurrent work across admin event operations, mock provider work, commissioner contest setup, and team entry can use one consistent live tracker.
close_reason: Completed. Active module lanes were mapped into Beads epics during the project-wide rollout.

title: Use Beads for stable refinement question IDs
description: Adopt Beads project-wide as the stable ID layer for active refinement questions and decisions on larger cross-module discussions so chat numbering does not drift as answers and follow-up questions evolve.
close_reason: Completed. Stable refinement question IDs were adopted on larger cross-module lanes such as the CEFI decision set.

title: Project-wide Beads workflow rollout
description: Expand Beads from the contest pilot to the repo-wide live workflow layer. Use Beads for active slice status, dependencies, and stable refinement question/decision IDs on larger cross-module lanes while keeping requirements/, tech-specs/, and plans/ in their existing roles.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Create contest page has no usable empty state when no events are available
description: When the event list is empty, the page just leaves the event picker blank and disables the Create contest button. It does not show the product-approved explanatory state telling the commissioner that no eligible real-world event is currently available. Evidence: clients/poolmaster/src/features/contests/create-contest-page.tsx lines 1297-1312 disable submit when eventsQuery.data is empty, but the only dedicated messaging block at lines 1399-1404 covers load errors, not the normal no-events-available state.
close_reason: Create contest now shows a dedicated no-events-available state when no contest-eligible golf events are ready, instead of only disabling the submit button.

title: Deepen mock-provider scoring correction propagation coverage
notes: Deferred enhancement. First-pass latest-truth overwrite propagation is in place; deeper scoring-correction replay realism is a follow-on hardening lane, not current required work.

title: Deepen PoolMaster-derived budget pricing coverage
notes: Closed as redundant. Budget pricing is derived in PoolMaster from provider valuation inputs, and the core valuation/derivation direction has already been implemented in the current contest/event feed lane. No separate mock-provider budget-pricing lane is needed.
close_reason: Invalid lane: budget pricing is derived in PoolMaster from provider valuation inputs, not a mock-provider coverage track.

title: Build root-admin provider sync run page
description: Replace the /root-admin placeholder in PoolMaster with a thin read-only provider sync-run visibility page backed by the generated admin sync-run contract and focused frontend tests.
notes: Frontend root-admin sync-run page implemented locally with focused tests and eslint clean. Package-level @poolmaster/poolmaster typecheck is currently blocked by pre-existing unrelated local errors in clients/poolmaster/src/features/contests/contest-detail-page.tsx (missing getContestEntry export, rank/string drift, isTied property mismatch).
close_reason: Root-admin provider sync run page is implemented as a read-only PoolMaster surface with focused tests and validation.

title: Synthesize upcoming tournament fields, rankings, and odds
description: Use completed tournaments, stable rankings, and historical reference signals to create believable but deterministic mock participant fields, rankings, and odds for upcoming golf events in the mock-provider season catalog.
notes: Goal is not perfect realism; goal is stable, explainable season-long mock data that can drive contest tiers, pricing, and entry flows before real providers are live.
close_reason: The golf season catalog already includes deterministic upcoming-event fields, rankings, and odds for future 2026 tournaments, and scenario validation is passing.

title: Source 2026 golf event schedule for mock provider
description: Compile the 2026 golf tournament schedule for the first mock-provider season from real-world references, then translate it into deterministic event scenario files for the mock provider.
notes: Completed first-pass 2026 golf season schedule sourcing and encoded a deterministic season backbone into the golf scenario while preserving the stable scenario id for compatibility.

title: Deploy mock provider to QA and wire CI usage
description: Add the infrastructure and CI/CD wiring so the mock provider can run as a dedicated QA service and be used by QA/manual flows and future ingestion/browser verification lanes.
notes: Completed QA deploy and CI/CD wiring for the mock provider: Dockerfile, CI build job, QA publish/deploy wiring, Terraform ECS/service discovery resources, and provider-binding env configuration at the infrastructure layer.

title: Harden mock provider event-first scenario model
description: Refine the JSON scenario contract so it represents season schedules, event metadata, fields, rankings, odds, updates, and results in an event-first way that maps cleanly onto the ingestion provider port without needing a database.
notes: Completed event-first mock provider scenario hardening with field/detail endpoints, staged updates, deep validation, scenario tests, and refreshed generated package artifacts.

title: Add ingestion and scoring verification against mock provider
description: Build the dedicated test lane that exercises ingestion normalization, contest field derivation inputs, and downstream scoring/result propagation against the mock provider scenarios end to end.
notes: Added integration verification scaffolding for the event-first mock provider contract, including a helper that boots the real provider app and a passing integration suite that bridges the adapter into ingestion persistence and ranking persistence.

title: Seed golf season scenario catalog in mock provider
description: Expand the mock provider from a few sample tournaments into a durable golf season catalog with realistic event schedules, participant fields, rankings, odds, and update/result scenarios for Masters, PGA Championship, and adjacent events.
notes: Still JSON-only, no database. Data can be curated manually from real-world schedules and historical references, then stabilized as deterministic scenario files.
close_reason: Golf season scenario catalog is seeded and validated, including real 2026 schedule anchors plus deterministic upcoming fields, rankings, and odds.

title: Evolve mock provider toward event-first season scenarios
description: Extend the mock contest feed provider to act as the durable event-first source for golf season schedules, fields, rankings, odds, and update scenarios that can drive ingestion and manual QA.
notes: Deferred enhancement umbrella. Event-first season scenarios and first-pass realism are implemented; remaining deeper scoring-correction propagation realism is intentionally deferred.

title: Retrospect workflow after each completed slice
description: Apply the new repo rule to briefly review each completed slice for workflow friction and capture recommended process improvements when they would improve future delivery efficiency.
close_reason: Completed. Slice retrospectives and workflow recommendations were incorporated into the working rules and used during this implementation lane.

title: Review Beads workflow impact on plans task tables
description: After one real contest-lane slice is completed in Beads, review whether active plans should keep full markdown task tables or shift toward lighter narrative context while Beads owns the live execution status.
close_reason: Completed. The Beads-vs-plans review was performed and repo rules/docs were updated so requirements are product truth, tech-specs are technical truth, and plans are execution context only.

title: Track remaining contest entry UX cleanup
description: Capture the next contest-entry UI refinements after the CTA-copy slice lands, including any remaining team-context consistency work that stays within the approved first-pass entry model.
close_reason: Completed. Contest entry UX cleanup landed in the tiered golf entry polish slice and locked-entry read-only detail work.

title: Normalize contest entry launch CTA copy
description: Update League Home, Team Home, and Contest Detail so the contest-entry launch action consistently says 'Create entry'. Keep rename available only on existing entry cards and do not introduce additional post-entry action labels yet.
close_reason: Closed

title: Contest lane Beads pilot
description: Pilot Beads as the live execution-state layer for the current contest implementation lane while keeping plans/ as the narrative context source. Start with the active team-context contest entry work and use the pilot to evaluate whether markdown task tables should eventually be reduced.
close_reason: all steps complete
